question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / EcrecoverAuthorizer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploit EcrecoverAuthorizer's permissionless submission (anyone can call setIsAuthorized with a valid sig) under PRECONDITIONS where attacker obtained a signed Authorization off-chain, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker submits authorization before the intended recipient does, gaining isAuthorized status, then immediately calls setIsRootRatified with a malicious root, violating INVARIANT that authorization submission must not be front-runnable to harmful effect, causing scoped impact: attacker front-runs legitimate authorization submission to ratify malicious root first? Proof idea: unit test asserting permissionless submission with valid sig always succeeds regardless of caller.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can an unprivileged TAKER fill an offer from a ratified root where the offer's offer.tick is not a multiple of the market's tickSpacing under PRECONDITIONS where root was ratified before tickSpacing was decreased, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker calls take() with offer.tick not aligned to new tickSpacing, violating INVARIANT that TickNotAccessible check in take() must block misaligned ticks, causing scoped impact: SetterRatifier does not enforce tick alignment, relying solely on Midnight.take() guard? Proof idea: unit test with tickSpacing decreased after root ratification, assert take reverts with TickNotAccessible.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can an unprivileged TAKER use ratifierData with a proof array of length > 20 under PRECONDITIONS where HashLib.offerTreeTypeHash reverts for height > 20 (only used in EcrecoverRatifier), but SetterRatifier.isRatified calls HashLib.isLeaf which supports up to 256 levels, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker submits proof.length=21 to SetterRatifier.isRatified, violating INVARIANT that tree height must be bounded, causing scoped impact: SetterRatifier allows height-21+ trees while EcrecoverRatifier does not, creating inconsistent security guarantees? Proof idea: unit test with proof.length=21 in SetterRatifier, assert isLeaf succeeds if root matches.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can an unprivileged TAKER fill an offer from a ratified root after the maker has been liquidated and their position is empty under PRECONDITIONS where root is still ratified and offer.buy=true (maker is buyer/lender), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker calls take() post-liquidation with maker's buy offer, violating INVARIANT that maker must have sufficient assets to fulfill the offer, causing scoped impact: maker callback or direct transfer fails if maker has no loan tokens, but SetterRatifier does not check maker solvency? Proof idea: unit test with liquidated maker, assert take reverts at token transfer step not at ratification.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / Midnight.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged AUTHORIZED_OPERATOR call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for a root containing offers with offer.maker != maker under PRECONDITIONS where operator holds isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator ratifies root where leaf offers have offer.maker=otherMaker, taker submits take() with offer.maker=maker and same root, violating INVARIANT that isRootRatified[offer.maker][root] must match the maker in the offer, causing scoped impact: root ratified for maker A is checked against offer.maker=A but leaves contain offers for maker B? Proof idea: unit test asserting isRatified checks isRootRatified[offer.maker][root] where offer.maker comes from the submitted offer struct.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / EcrecoverAuthorizer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploit the fact that EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized emits an event before calling Midnight.setIsAuthorized under PRECONDITIONS where event emission order matters for off-chain monitoring, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker submits authorization, event fires, but Midnight.setIsAuthorized call reverts (e.g., if Midnight is paused or has a bug), violating INVARIANT that event emission must match actual state change, causing scoped impact: off-chain systems believe authorization was granted but on-chain state was not updated? Proof idea: unit test asserting Midnight.setIsAuthorized is called atomically with event emission.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can an unprivileged TAKER submit ratifierData with malformed ABI encoding under PRECONDITIONS where abi.decode(ratifierData, (bytes32, uint256, bytes32[])) reverts on malformed input, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker calls take() with ratifierData=hex'deadbeef', violating INVARIANT that malformed ratifierData must revert cleanly, causing scoped impact: if abi.decode reverts with a non-standard error, take() reverts with RatifierFail masking the root cause? Proof idea: fuzz ratifierData with random bytes and assert take always reverts (never succeeds) with malformed data.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified] Can an unprivileged TAKER fill an offer from a ratified root where the offer's offer.market.enterGate or offer.market.liquidatorGate differs from the actual deployed market under PRECONDITIONS where root was ratified for offers with gate=G1 but taker submits offer with gate=G2, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker calls take() with modified gate addresses, violating INVARIANT that hashOffer must bind gate configuration, causing scoped impact: taker fills offer in a market with different gate restrictions than maker intended? Proof idea: unit test asserting hashMarket with different enterGate values produces different hashes and isLeaf returns false.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / Midnight.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged AUTHORIZED_OPERATOR call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) and then immediately call Midnight.setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) to cancel all offers in the group under PRECONDITIONS where operator holds isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator ratifies root then cancels consumption group, violating INVARIANT that maker must control both ratification and consumption group state, causing scoped impact: operator can selectively enable/disable maker's offers by toggling root ratification and consumed state? Proof idea: unit test asserting operator can call both setIsRootRatified and setConsumed on behalf of maker.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Signature replay, nonce desync, deadline edge case, or EIP-712 domain mistake that affects delegated authorization'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged TAKER exploit the absence of chain ID in HashLib.hashOffer under PRECONDITIONS where the same SetterRatifier contract is deployed at the same address on two chains (e.g., via CREATE2) and maker ratified a root on chain A, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker on chain B submits take() with the same offer and ratifierData, but isRootRatified[maker][root] is false on chain B (root was only ratified on chain A), violating INVARIANT that ratification state is chain-local, causing scoped impact: taker on chain B cannot exploit chain A ratification since isRootRatified is on-chain state, but confirms no cross-chain replay risk exists? Proof idea: differential test with two chain forks, assert isRootRatified is independent per chain.
